# Getting Started

Bootstrap projects, choose active workspaces, manage targets, and keep local Kirimana project scaffolding current.

## kiri init

First-run DW project bootstrap.

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri init [OPTIONS] NAME
```

### Arguments

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `NAME` | Project name (also the directory name) | yes |  |  |

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--path` | Parent directory for the new project | no | . |  |
| `--silver-technique` | Silver modelling technique: flat \| data_vault \| kimball_dimensional. Required; locked after first silver contract. | no |  |  |
| `--layout` | Project file layout: per_domain \| per_source | no | per_domain |  |
| `--adapter` | Primary target adapter | no | localduckdb |  |
| `--mode` | DPA mode: contract (default, user-owned contracts/) \| auto (Kiri-owned hidden .kiri/contracts/) \| hybrid (both). | no | contract |  |
| `--target-name` | Name of the primary target | no | dev |  |
| `--no-git` | Skip git init + initial commit | no | False |  |
| `--remote` | Add this URL as origin after the initial commit (e.g. git@github.com:org/repo.git) | no |  |  |
| `--push` | Run `git push -u origin <branch>` after adding the remote (requires --remote and auth) | no | False |  |
| `--github` | After scaffold + git-init + set-active, create the GitHub remote repo and push the initial commit so the scaffolded per-project CI runs automatically. A convenience - the emitted git + GitHub Actions still work with a manually-wired remote. | no | False |  |
| `--private`, `--public` | With --github: create the repo private (default - DW projects are sensitive) or public (explicit opt-out). | no | True |  |

### kiri project checksum

Print the SHA-256 of the project's kiri.yml.

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri project checksum [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

### kiri project commit

Atomic kiri.yml commit (kiriapp...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri project commit [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--content` | Path to a file containing the kiri.yml content to write | yes |  |  |
| `--project`, `-p` | Project directory | no |  |  |
| `--checksum` | SHA-256 hex of the kiri.yml the caller loaded; commit refuses if the on-disk hash differs (optimistic lock). Skip to disable conflict detection. | no |  |  |

### kiri project patch

Propose a kiri.yml patch.

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri project patch [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--op` | Patch op: add_target \| remove_target \| set_default_target \| set_silver_technique \| add_domain \| remove_domain \| set_vault_provider \| set_naming_override \| set_reconcile_tolerance \| set_governance_mode | yes |  |  |
| `--args` | JSON-encoded args dict for the op | no | {} |  |
| `--project`, `-p` | Project directory | no |  |  |
| `--apply` | Write the patched kiri.yml to disk (default: dry-run, prints proposal only) | no | False |  |

### kiri project scaffold

Scaffold a new project (kiriapp...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri project scaffold [OPTIONS] NAME
```

### Arguments

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `NAME` | Project name (also the directory name) | yes |  |  |

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--path` | Parent directory for the new project | no | . |  |
| `--silver-technique` | Required: flat \| data_vault \| kimball_dimensional | no |  |  |
| `--layout` | per_domain \| per_source | no | per_domain |  |
| `--adapter` | Primary target adapter (default localduckdb) | no | localduckdb |  |
| `--target-name` | Name of the primary target | no | dev |  |
| `--no-git` | Skip git init | no | False |  |
| `--ci` | Add a GitHub Actions workflow | no | False |  |
| `--overwrite` | Overwrite existing project files (DESTRUCTIVE) | no | False |  |

### kiri project status

Print the active-project identity +...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri project status [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--project`, `-p` | Project directory | no |  |  |
| `--format` | json \| text | no | json |  |
| `--ledger` | Path to a markdown ledger (e.g. PROGRESS.md). When set, Kiri stamps per-chunk status into the file between its sentinels. Idempotent - a no-op when the rendered table is byte-identical to what is on disk. | no |  |  |

## kiri reload

Rebuild the Delta bronze table by...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri reload [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--project` | Project directory | no |  |  |
| `--target`, `-t` | Named profile to use (dev\|test\|prod). Falls back to KIRIMANA_TARGET or the project's default_target. | no |  |  |
| `--source` | Source name, e.g. air_quality | yes |  |  |
| `--table` | Table name, e.g. measurements | yes |  |  |

```bash
kiri version's conversion rules.
```

```text
Usage: kiri scaffold [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

None.

### Subcommands

- [`kiri scaffold bronze`](#kiri-scaffold-bronze) - Emit one bronze contract per source table.

### kiri scaffold all

Run a closed-menu plan that fans out...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri scaffold all [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--plan` | Closed menu: minimal, pit-only, plan-a-1to1, plan-b-kimball, plan-b-layer. | yes |  |  |
| `--output` | Output root directory. | yes |  |  |
| `--inventory` | Upstream inventory JSON. Required for every plan EXCEPT ``--plan minimal`` (which synthesises a generic example). | no |  |  |
| `--entity-map` | Entity-map YAML. Required for every plan EXCEPT ``--plan minimal``. | no |  |  |
| `--target-catalog` |  | no | main |  |
| `--landing-zone` | Bronze landing-zone template (substitutes {catalog}/{source}/{entity}). | no | /Volumes/{catalog}/bronze_landing/{source}/{entity}/ |  |
| `--mart-lineage` | Mart lineage JSON (required for plan-b-kimball). | no |  |  |
| `--routing-map` | Routing YAML (required for plan-b-kimball). | no |  |  |
| `--silver-pattern` | Raw-silver pattern for plan-b-kimball. Closed menu: scd2, type1, append-only, snapshot. | no | scd2 |  |
| `--ingest-mechanism` |  | no | auto_loader+dlt |  |
| `--classification-baseline` |  | no | confidential |  |
| `--source-system` |  | no | upstream |  |
| `--domain` |  | no | platform |  |
| `--owner` |  | no | platform-owner@example.com |  |
| `--locale-pack` | Classification cue overrides. | no |  |  |

### kiri scaffold bronze

Emit one bronze contract per source table.

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri scaffold bronze [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--inventory` | Upstream inventory JSON. | yes |  |  |
| `--entity-map` | YAML mapping source-FQN → entity slug. | yes |  |  |
| `--target-catalog` | Destination catalog name. | yes |  |  |
| `--landing-zone` | Landing-zone template. Substitutes {catalog}, {source}, {entity}. | yes |  |  |
| `--output` | Directory to write bronze YAML into. | yes |  |  |
| `--ingest-mechanism` | Closed menu: auto_loader+dlt, auto_loader, copy_into, streaming_table, rest_pull, landing_zone_file. | no | auto_loader+dlt |  |
| `--classification-baseline` | Fallback classification tier when no cue matches. | no | confidential |  |
| `--source-system` |  | no | upstream |  |
| `--source-system-kind` |  | no | rest_api |  |
| `--domain` |  | no | platform |  |
| `--owner` | Contract owner. Operator MUST override the default. | no | platform-owner@example.com |  |
| `--locale-pack` | YAML file with classification cue overrides per tier. | no |  |  |

### kiri scaffold mart

Emit a mart contract with the legacy...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri scaffold mart [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--upstream-layer` |  | yes |  |  |
| `--upstream-entity` |  | yes |  |  |
| `--upstream-catalog` |  | yes |  |  |
| `--output` | Output YAML path. | yes |  |  |
| `--from-legacy-view` | Legacy view FQN (used in metadata; informational unless --column-list is omitted and --view-sql supplied). | no |  |  |
| `--column-list` | Text file with one column per line. Authoritative when supplied. | no |  |  |
| `--view-sql` | SQL file holding the legacy view definition; columns are extracted via sqlglot. | no |  |  |
| `--parity-required` | Closed menu: exact, approximate, advisory. | no | exact |  |
| `--target-catalog` |  | no |  |  |
| `--target-schema` |  | no | mart |  |
| `--mart-name` |  | no |  |  |
| `--domain` |  | no | platform |  |
| `--owner` |  | no | platform-owner@example.com |  |
| `--sql` | Path to a SQL file holding the mart SELECT body. Required unless --allow-placeholder is set. F14 refuses placeholder marts by default - they mask missing business logic. | no |  |  |
| `--allow-placeholder` | Opt-in escape hatch for prototyping: emit `SELECT 1 AS placeholder` body. Off by default. | no | False |  |

### kiri scaffold silver

Emit silver contract(s) - pattern-driven...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri scaffold silver [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--output` | Output path (file when --pattern, dir when --from-marts). | yes |  |  |
| `--pattern` | Closed menu: scd2, type1, append-only, snapshot. Mutually exclusive with --from-marts. | no |  |  |
| `--upstream-catalog` |  | no |  |  |
| `--upstream-entity` |  | no |  |  |
| `--primary-key` | Comma-separated PK columns. | no |  |  |
| `--scd2-tracking-columns` | Comma-separated columns whose change triggers a new SCD2 version. | no |  |  |
| `--from-marts` | Enable mart-driven column inference. Requires --contracts + --routing-map. | no | False |  |
| `--contracts` | Mart contracts directory (when --from-marts). | no |  |  |
| `--mart-lineage` | Mart lineage JSON (when --from-marts). | no |  |  |
| `--routing-map` | Entity routing YAML (when --from-marts). | no |  |  |
| `--target-catalog` |  | no |  |  |
| `--target-schema` |  | no |  |  |
| `--domain` |  | no | platform |  |
| `--owner` |  | no | platform-owner@example.com |  |

## kiri upgrade

Release upgrade lifecycle.

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri upgrade [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--check` | Read-only pre-flight dry-run. Required in this release. | no | False |  |
| `--project`, `-p` | Project directory | no |  |  |
| `--target`, `-t` | Named target from kiri.yml to resolve under the new schema. | no |  |  |
| `--to` | Target release version (e.g. v0.19.0). Recorded in the report; the check always validates against the schema this kiri build ships. | no |  |  |
| `--format` | json \| text | no | json |  |

### Subcommands

- [`kiri upgrade rollback`](#kiri-upgrade-rollback) - Reverse an upgrade: Helm revert → DB...
- [`kiri upgrade snapshot`](#kiri-upgrade-snapshot) - Capture a restorable pre-upgrade Postgres...

### kiri upgrade rollback

Reverse an upgrade: Helm revert → DB...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri upgrade rollback [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--snapshot`, `-s` | Directory holding the snapshot manifest + dump produced by `kiri upgrade snapshot`. | no | .kiri-snapshots |  |
| `--database-url` | Automation Postgres URL. Defaults to $DATABASE_URL. | no |  |  |
| `--release` | Helm release name. | no | kirimana |  |
| `--namespace`, `-n` | Kubernetes namespace. | no | kirimana |  |
| `--revision` | Helm revision to roll back to. Omit for the immediately-previous revision. | no |  |  |
| `--dry-run` | Print the exact helm/pg_restore/migrate plan; write nothing, run nothing. | no | False |  |

### kiri upgrade snapshot

Capture a restorable pre-upgrade Postgres...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri upgrade snapshot [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--out`, `-o` | Directory the dump + manifest are written to. | no | .kiri-snapshots |  |
| `--database-url` | Automation Postgres URL. Defaults to $DATABASE_URL (same resolution as `python -m kirimana_automation.db.migrate`). | no |  |  |
| `--tag` | Human label folded into the artifact name. Defaults to a UTC timestamp. | no |  |  |
| `--dry-run` | Print the exact pg_dump invocation + manifest plan; write nothing, run nothing. | no | False |  |

## kiri use

Set, show, or clear the active DW project...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri use [OPTIONS] [DIRECTORY]
```

### Arguments

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `DIRECTORY` | Project directory to make active. Omit to show the current one. | no |  |  |

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--clear` | Forget the persisted active project. | no | False |  |
